keyEvents.impact: Two decades of military rule left lasting wariness of the armed forces in politics

keyEvents.event: Redemocratization and end of military rule
keyEvents.impact: Civilian government restored; 1988 constitution entrenched broad social rights
Monetary stabilization created the foundation for modern Brazilian economic credibility
Bolsa Família lifted millions from poverty; Brazil pursued South-South diplomacy and BRICS leadership
Sweeping investigations discredited the political class and fueled anti-establishment polarization
Deepened left-right rupture that culminated in the bitterly contested 2022 election
Bolsonarista riot tested democratic institutions; polarization remains the central domestic fault line

history.narrative: A continental power that sees itself as the natural leader of South America and a voice of the Global South, Brazil pursues prestige through diplomacy, trade, and multilateral institutions rather than force. It guards Amazon sovereignty jealously and refuses to choose sides between Washington, Beijing, and Moscow, preferring autonomy and a seat at every table.

redLines.description: Foreign military presence or internationalization of the Amazon
Punitive sanctions or trade coercion over environmental or agricultural policy
Foreign interference in Brazilian elections or judicial processes

internalDivisions.name: Lulista left versus Bolsonarista right polarization
Agribusiness expansion versus environmental and indigenous protection

goals.description: Lead the Global South and secure a permanent UN Security Council seat
Grow agricultural and industrial exports while reducing poverty and inequality
Assert sovereign control over the Amazon and its resources
Keep South America stable and free of great-power military competition
